title: Slide Design Principles
subtitle: make your presentation beautiful & clear!
overview: This session introduces students to the basic principles of slide design. Students learn how to create attractive, readable, and engaging presentations using colors, fonts, images, alignment, and minimal text.

images._note: Add screenshots of good and bad slide examples, student presentations, font examples, color combinations, and classroom activities.

gallery.caption: A clean slide with less text and clear visuals

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Example of a crowded slide
gallery.caption: Too much text makes slides difficult to read

keyTopics: Visual hierarchy — title bigger than content

activity.title: Design Your Own Presentation Slide
steps: Create a title slide on your favorite topic
Use only 5–6 keywords on the slide
Choose matching colors and readable fonts
Discuss what makes the slide attractive

quiz.q: What is the most important rule in slide design?
quiz.explanation: Simple and clean slides are easier to understand and look professional.

quiz.q: Which font style is easiest to read in presentations?
options: Simple fonts like Arial or Calibri
quiz.explanation: Simple fonts improve readability for the audience.

quiz.q: How much text should a slide contain?
quiz.explanation: Slides should contain short points instead of long paragraphs.

quiz.q: Why are images used in presentations?
quiz.explanation: Images help people understand concepts quickly and make slides interesting.

quiz.q: What is a good color combination for readability?
quiz.explanation: Dark text on a light background provides good contrast and readability.

quiz.q: What does alignment mean in slide design?
quiz.explanation: Alignment keeps slides organized and visually balanced.

quiz.q: Why should animations be used carefully?
options: Too many animations distract the audience
quiz.explanation: Excessive animations can distract viewers from the main message.

options: Using bigger titles and smaller content
quiz.explanation: Visual hierarchy helps viewers identify important information quickly.

quiz.q: What should be avoided in presentations?
options: Crowded slides with too much information
quiz.explanation: Too much content makes slides difficult to read and understand.

quiz.q: Which tool can be used to create presentations?
quiz.explanation: All these tools can be used to create beautiful presentations.

quiz.q: What is the benefit of using consistent colors and fonts?
quiz.explanation: Consistency improves the professional appearance of presentations.

quiz.q: What is better for a presentation slide?
quiz.explanation: One idea per slide keeps the audience focused.
